q: What tables exist in this database?
sql:
SELECT table_name
FROM information_schema.tables
WHERE table_schema = 'MEDICAL'
  AND table_catalog = 'CPT_DEMO'
ORDER BY table_name;
answer: The database’s MEDICAL schema contains four tables: CPT_CODES, PATIENTS, VISITS, and VISIT_PROCEDURES. Together, these likely store procedure codes, patient information, visit records, and the procedures performed during each visit.

sql:
SELECT
    patients.patient_id,
    patients.first_name,
    patients.last_name,
    patients.date_of_birth,
    patients.age,
    patients.gender,
    patients.insurance_id
FROM cpt_demo.medical.patients AS patients;
answer: There is one patient in the system: James Carter (patient ID PAT-001), a 30-year-old male born on 1994-03-15, with insurance ID INS-78432901.

q: What procedures were billed for visit VIS-001?
sql:
SELECT
  visit_procedures.procedure_id,
  visit_procedures.visit_id,
  visit_procedures.cpt_code,
  cpt_codes.description,
  cpt_codes.category,
  visit_procedures.units,
  visit_procedures.fee_charged,
  visit_procedures.reimbursement,
  visit_procedures.notes
FROM cpt_demo.medical.visit_procedures AS visit_procedures
LEFT JOIN cpt_demo.medical.cpt_codes AS cpt_codes
  ON visit_procedures.cpt_code = cpt_codes.cpt_code
WHERE visit_procedures.visit_id = 'VIS-001';
answer: For visit VIS-001, the patient was billed for an adult preventive checkup (age 18–39), a hepatitis B vaccine, and the associated vaccine administration fee.

q: What is the total charged vs reimbursed per visit?
sql:
SELECT
  visits.visit_id,
  visits.patient_id,
  visits.visit_date,
  SUM(visit_procedures.fee_charged) AS total_fee_charged,
  SUM(visit_procedures.reimbursement) AS total_reimbursement
FROM cpt_demo.medical.visits AS visits
JOIN cpt_demo.medical.visit_procedures AS visit_procedures
  ON visits.visit_id = visit_procedures.visit_id
GROUP BY
  visits.visit_id,
  visits.patient_id,
  visits.visit_date
ORDER BY
  visits.visit_date,
  visits.visit_id;
answer: For visit VIS-001 on 2024-06-10 by patient PAT-001, a total of $275.00 was charged and $220.00 was reimbursed. This means $55.00 of the charged amount was not reimbursed.

q: Which patient had the most expensive visit?
sql:
SELECT
    p.patient_id,
    p.first_name,
    p.last_name,
    v.visit_id,
    v.visit_date,
    SUM(vp.fee_charged) AS total_visit_fee
FROM cpt_demo.medical.visits v
JOIN cpt_demo.medical.patients p
    ON v.patient_id = p.patient_id
JOIN cpt_demo.medical.visit_procedures vp
    ON v.visit_id = vp.visit_id
GROUP BY
    p.patient_id,
    p.first_name,
    p.last_name,
    v.visit_id,
    v.visit_date
QUALIFY
    RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(vp.fee_charged) DESC) = 1;
answer: James Carter (patient ID PAT-001) had the most expensive visit, on 2024-06-10 (visit ID VIS-001), with total fees of $275.

q: Show the full billing summary with patient names
sql:
SELECT
    patients.patient_id,
    patients.first_name || ' ' || patients.last_name AS patient_name,
    patients.date_of_birth,
    patients.age,
    patients.gender,
    patients.insurance_id,
    visits.visit_id,
    visits.visit_date,
    visits.visit_type,
    visits.provider_name,
    visits.provider_npi,
    visits.diagnosis_code,
    visits.notes AS visit_notes,
    visit_procedures.procedure_id,
    visit_procedures.cpt_code,
    cpt_codes.description AS cpt_description,
    cpt_codes.category AS cpt_category,
    cpt_codes.base_fee,
    visit_procedures.units,
    visit_procedures.fee_charged,
    visit_procedures.reimbursement,
    visit_procedures.notes AS procedure_notes
FROM cpt_demo.medical.visit_procedures
JOIN cpt_demo.medical.visits
    ON visit_procedures.visit_id = visits.visit_id
JOIN cpt_demo.medical.patients
    ON visits.patient_id = patients.patient_id
JOIN cpt_demo.medical.cpt_codes
    ON visit_procedures.cpt_code = cpt_codes.cpt_code
ORDER BY
    patients.last_name,
    patients.first_name,
    visits.visit_date,
    visits.visit_id,
    visit_procedures.procedure_id;
answer: This report shows a single preventive care visit for patient James Carter on 2024-06-10 with Dr. Sarah Nguyen, during which he received a standard adult preventive exam plus a Hepatitis B vaccine and its administration. The summary includes his demographics, insurance, diagnosis, and detailed billing information (CPT codes, fees charged, and reimbursements) for each of the three billed procedures.
